mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate held at the zero lower bound compresses the equity discount rate and supports risk asset valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 20-day SPX returns falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 9.5% signals insufficient labor market recovery, depressing consumption expectations and corporate earnings forecasts, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 9.5%, positive 60-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously rising retail sales and a near-zero federal funds rate indicate a demand recovery financed by accommodative monetary policy, jointly predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS and FEDFUNDS in a bivariate regression fails to predict 20-day returns with a t-statistic above 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth predicts positive S&P 500 returns exclusively during early-cycle recoveries when rebounding factory output signals improving capacity utilization before margin compression sets in.
failure_condition: During early-cycle recoveries, INDPRO momentum exhibits a Rank IC below 0.04 for predicting 5-day forward returns over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly increases in housing starts signal a durable turnaround in residential construction and related downstream demand, forecasting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When housing starts rise for two straight months, negative 60-day forward returns occur in more than 45 percent of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in nonfarm payrolls lifts household income and consumption forecasts, translating into higher corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between month-over-month PAYEMS change and 5-day SPX returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation accelerating above 3 percent annualized over three months triggers fears of premature monetary tightening, increasing discount rates and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When CPI acceleration exceeds 3.0 percent annualized, positive 20-day SPX returns occur in more than 60 percent of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with falling unemployment confirms a synchronized supply-demand recovery, an interaction that forecasts strongly positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint INDPRO-UNRATE interaction term does not significantly predict 60-day returns in a multivariate regression at the 5 percent level.
